etymology.description: 老(老) (lǎo, "old") + 火(火) (huǒ, "fire") + 鍋(鍋) (guō, "pot")
etymology.interpretation: 老(老) (lǎo, "old") gives a sense of something traditional or well-established. 火(火) (huǒ, "fire") represents the cooking method involving an open flame or source of heat. 鍋(鍋) (guō, "pot") refers to the container used for cooking. Together, 老火鍋 (lǎo huǒ guō) suggests a traditional or authentic method of preparing hot pot, often associated with longstanding culinary customs and flavors.

examples.chinese: 我们今晚去吃老火锅(我們今晚去吃老火鍋)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen jīnwǎn qù chī lǎo huǒ guō.
examples.english: We are going to eat old hot pot tonight.

examples.chinese: 老火锅的味道很经典(老火鍋的味道很經典)。
examples.pinyin: Lǎo huǒ guō de wèidào hěn jīngdiǎn.
examples.english: The taste of old hot pot is very classic.

examples.chinese: 他最喜欢老火锅的辣味(他最喜歡老火鍋的辣味)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zuì xǐhuān lǎo huǒ guō de là wèi.
examples.english: He likes the spicy flavor of old hot pot the most.
